dialogos.texto_en: Ah, Fu-kun!\nOver here, over here!
N-Nino.\nDon't call out so loudly, we'll stand out.
You don't need to hide your embarrassment.\nYou're happy to see me, right?
(Oh no, this is bad.\n I'm getting caught up in Nino's wild world again.)
D-do you need something from me, Nino? If not%p-2;――
Fu-kun, aren't you tired from walking?\nHave some gelato with us.
Us?
Nino bought some for us.
Oh, Uesugi-kun? When did you get here?
*sigh*\nOh, Itsuki's here too, huh.
What's with that tone?\nIt's rude to sigh right in someone's face.
No, I'm just relieved. I'm glad you're here.
Huh?! W-what do you mean?!\nWhat are you talking about all the sudden?!
Thanks, Itsuki. This one's mine, right?
Ah, yes.\nHere you are, apple mango.
This looks delicious!\nI'm pretty excited.
(It certainly looks...delicious, right?\n Is that something you can tell just by looking?)
I heard they use locally grown fruits\nfor the gelato here.
That's one of the joys of traveling,\nenjoying local flavors, isn't it?
Well, that's true.\nThen maybe I'll try something new.
(The menu...\n Pineapple, mango, and salted milk?\n There are more flavors than I thought.)
I have no idea.\nWell, I guess vanilla is fine.
Hey, Fu-kun. Did you not hear what Itsuki said?
We should try something\nthat we can only have in Okinawa,\nsince we're here, and all that.
You saying that doesn't really help my decision.\nBy the way, what flavors did you guys get?
I got the apple mango and Okinawa strawberry flavors.
I got a scoop of purple sweet potato and Okinawa tofu,\nwith black honey as a topping.
Now I have no idea what to order.
Fu-kun, you're not good with this kind of thing,\nare you?\nIf you're not sure, why don't you try mine?
...Whaaa?!
N-Nino?!
Wh-what are you saying?!\nThat's gelato that you...
Teeehee, you're so cute when you're embarrassed.
I don't care.\nCome on, why don't you try it?
Please, DO care!
Oh my, you're so uptight, Itsuki.\nIt's just a little joke,\nthere's no need to be so angry.
There's a limit to jokes you know!
Come on, Uesugi-kun, please decide quickly.\nBefore things get more complicated!
Huh?\nThen, vanilla%p-2;――
Decide, on a flavor that can only be tasted here!
Okay...
Ah, that's...not really appropriate.
Why not?\nThere's no need to hold back, just eat it.
U-Uesugi-kun, don't you think it's a bit\ntoo complex of a flavor for you to handle?!
Do you think?\nI think it's simpler than your flavor, Itsuki.
Oh come on, you always ends up saying\nthat it's just 'good,' no matter what you eat!\nIsn't that right, Uesugi-kun?!
Uh, yeah, I guess so.
So that's why, you should hurry up and decide,\nand buy some gelato!
Why, again?
That's yours, isn't it?\nI'll buy my own portion.\nThanks for your consideration though.
Hmm.\nWell, fine then.
I still recommend the fruity flavors.\nThis apple-mango is REALLY delicious.
Fruity flavors, huh.\nIf it's a fruit from Okinawa...
Oh, there's a good one.
Come on, Fu-kun.\nHurry up and buy it,\nor we'll finish eating without you.
Alright.
I tried the Shikuwasa flavor.
Ah! That's the one I was curious about.\nWhat's it taste like?
It's sour, but it's good.
I knew it, it's just 'good,' like always.\nI had a feeling.
No, when I say something's good,\nI mean it's delicious, you know?
...That's enough.\nMy head's starting to spin.
By the way, did you know that gelato\nhas less fat, and is relatively healthy?
Really!
Mmh...
Fruit-based gelato, especially, contains nutrients\nwhich allow you to beat the heat, like vitamin C.
It's healthy, delicious, and good for your skin.\nGelato is amazing, isn't it?
Don't you think it's amazing, Uesugi-kun?!
Calm down and let me eat.\nYou may have finished eating already,\nbut I'm not quite done yet.
Itsuki, you had a double scoop of purple sweet potato\nand Okinawa tofu with black honey topping, right?
Yes, that's right.
There's no fruit in there at all, is there?
Ooh...
On top of that, there's a difference in\nfat and calories. Quite the difference.\nToppings really add up calories too, you know?
Oooohh...
It all comes down to how much you eat.
WAAAAH!\nEven Nino's being so COLD to me!
(Nino really is merciless, isn't she...)
